title: Comment and uncomment code
text: Comment and uncomment code You can comment and uncomment codes in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. You need to right-click on a code and click Comment on the popup menu to comment out a line when there is no selection or a complete selection of codes. Similarly, you need to right-click inside the commented code or a selection and click Uncomment to remove a comment from the code and make the code part of the program flow. The Uniface IDE editor supports the Comment and Uncomment feature with the following types of codes: ProcScript JavaScript in ProcScript JavaScript in HTML CSS HTML Additionally, you can use the default shortcut keys defined for Comment and Uncomment feature in the [userkeys] section of the usys.ini file. The default shortcut keys defined in the usys.ini file are Ctrl+K to comment out a code and Ctrl+Shift+K to uncomment a code as defined in the following block: Explain this code [USERKEYS] code.comment=Ctrl+K code.uncomment=Ctrl+Shift+K Copy Note: You can change the default key combinations defined in the usys.ini file as per your preference. Examples Comment in ProcScript When the JavaScript keyword is commented out, the subsequent code is treated as ProcScript . The ProcScript comment character ; is used when this code is commented. For example: Explain this code ;javascript ; function myFunction(p1, p2) { ; return p1 * p2; ; } ;endjavascript Copy Comment in JavaScript inside ProcScript The code within the JavaScript and endjavaScript keywords is treated as JavaScript . The JavaScript comment characters // are used when this code is commented. For example: Explain this code javascript // function myFunction(p1, p2) { // return p1 * p2; // } endjavascript Copy Comment in JavaScript inside HTML The code within the <script> and </script> tags is treated as JavaScript . The JavaScript comment characters // are used when this code is commented. For example: Explain this code <sctipt> // function myFunction(p1, p2) { // return p1 * p2; // } </script> Copy Comment in CSS inside HTML The code within the <style> and </style> tags is treated as CSS. The CSS block comment characters /* and */ are used when this code is commented. For example (Single line): Explain this code <style> /*p.dotted {border-style: dotted;}*/ p.dashed {border-style: dashed;} </style> Copy For example (Multiple lines): Explain this code <style> /*p.dotted {border-style: dotted;} p.dashed {border-style: dashed;}*/ </style> Copy Comment in HTML The code within the <html> and </html> tags is treated as HTML. The HTML block comment characters <!-- and --> are used when this code is commented. For example (Single line): Explain this code <head> <!-- <meta name="default" content="Uniface 10" />--> <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="../css/uniface.css" /> </head> Copy For example (Multiple lines): Explain this code <head> <!-- <meta name="default" content="Uniface 10" /> <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="../css/uniface.css" />--> </head> Copy Note: Adding a comment inside a comment in HTML is not allowed.
